Title: Zed
Age: Current31 – 36 years old Timeline Born961 AN – 966 AN Edit
Occupation: Heir to the Eye of Twilight

children.title: Early life
children.textContent: The master assassin who would become known as Zed was born as Govos to a peasant family. When he was four years old, his father abandoned his family. Govos spent his days working at the Kinkou Order's Monastery, dreaming of becoming a legendary warrior, though few ever spoke to him. One evening, Master Kusho, the Eye of Twilight, took pity on him and invited him to spar with his son, Shen. Though Shen easily won each bout, the boy continued to rise and demand rematches. Watching closely, Kusho offered guidance on how to counter Shen's strikes, until Govos finally managed to land a hit. Impressed by his determination, Kusho took Govos under his wing. Shen and Govos trained together thereafter, growing as close as brothers. Govos's mother searched for him at the monastery and begged him to return, but he refused to acknowledge her and allowed her to be dragged away.

children.title: Hunt for the Golden Demon
children.textContent:
Two years after Govos had been taken in by Kusho, the Council of Zhyun sent an envoy to Great Master Kusho begging for aid in stopping the infamous killer known as the Golden Demon. Kusho agreed, taking Govos and Shen with him, and began a covert investigation. Together, they tended to families shattered by the killings, dissected the horrific crime scenes, and looked for connections between the murders.

As they made their way to Kotha Village, Shen and Govos spoke about how Shen's father had arranged for him to marry Yevnai. Shen was nervous, though he loved Yevnai, he was unsure whether she felt the same way, but Govos reassured him that she did. When they came upon the town, they found it massacred by the demon. They tried to tend to the survivors, and Govos worked desperately to save as many lives as he could, but the scale of the carnage shook him to his core. When they were finished, Kusho tried to comfort Govos and imparted to him the lesson of the scales: sometimes you must lose one to save the many. Impressed by his resolve, Kusho named Govos his successor as the Eye of Twilight and granted him a new name, Usan.

Their investigation spanned four long years, leaving all three men changed. Shen, once known for his wit and humor, grew increasingly somber; Usan, the brightest star of Kusho's temple, began to struggle with his studies; and Kusho's famous red mane turned white. When Kusho finally discovered a pattern in the killings, he realized they were not hunting a demon, but a wicked man or vastaya. Believing the matter to be beyond the Kinkou's mandate, he sought to hand off the investigation. However, Shen and Usan refused to turn back after all they had endured, and convinced him to continue.

Three years into the investigation, on the eve of the Spirit Blossom Festival in Jyom Pass, Kusho disguised himself as a renowned calligrapher to blend in among the visiting artists, while Shen and Usan carefully prepared a trap. Usan chased the Golden Demon through the harbor, but was ultimately ambushed and knocked unconscious. When Usan awoke, the murderer revealed that he had lured Shen's fiancée Yevnai to the festival and had prepared an array of traps to turn her into his next masterpiece. Unbeknownst to Shen, Usan and Yevnai had developed feelings for one another, and the demon penned a letter to her under his name, inviting her to meet him.

Desperate, Usan begged for her life and revealed that the amulet he carried allowed his master to track him. As the demon prepared for his performance, Shen burst through the wall to save Usan, revealing that he had intentionally allowed himself to be captured as bait. Usan seized one of the killer's blades and attempted to strike him down, but Kusho intervened, insisting they take the killer back alive.

In the aftermath, Kusho was proven right, and the so-called demon was revealed to be a human stagehand named Khada Jhin. Kusho decided that Jhin should be imprisoned within the monastery prison at Tuula. While both Shen and Usan believed Jhin deserved a far harsher punishment, Shen accepted his father's judgment and attempted to comfort a bitter and resentful Usan. Usan, however, was deeply disturbed and haunted by the horrors he had witnessed. Unable to understand or accept their master's mercy, resentment took root within him, and hatred bloomed in his heart.

children.title: Rise of the Order of Shadow
children.textContent:
When Noxus began its invasion of the First Lands, Masters Kennen, Kusho, and Mayym ruled to not involve the Kinkou in the war. Shen secretly began sneaking out to train soldiers and attack enemy supply lines, while Usan helped cover for him by deliberately throwing his matches to make it appear as though Shen was keeping up with his training. Usan, on the other hand, had grown disillusioned and refused to let lofty notions of balance stand in the way of punishing evil. Usan ventured deep into the Kinkou temple's catacombs, where he discovered an ornate black box. When he peered inside, ancient shadow magic enveloped his mind, feeding his bitterness with contempt for the weak. Returning to the light of the temple, he came face to face with Great Master Kusho and demanded that the Kinkou strike the Noxian invaders with every means at their disposal. When Kusho refused, Usan turned his back on the order and left. Unbound by the Kinkou's doctrine, Usan raised a following of warriors to resist Noxus and urged them to embrace the fervor of war. Yet he soon realized that his own abilities would never match his ambitions.

During the siege of Thanze, Usan—now calling himself Zed—struggled to defend the city from the encroaching Noxian army. With Noxus poised to breach the walls by morning, Zed resolved to claim the power necessary to stop them. He led his followers back to the Great Kinkou Temple of Navori in Thanjuul to confront Master Kusho, and demanded the Tears of the Shadow. Kusho refused and warned that the Tears were not of this world and were too dangerous to wield. He laid down his weapon and pleaded with his former student to walk with him to see the Tears' evil and destroy it. Zed entered the catacombs with Kusho and, by nightfall, returned alone. He claimed that he had slayed the Kinkou's master and took the black box for himself, now wielding its shadow magic. Zed led his followers in a bloody coup, slaughtering the Kinkou and seizing their temple, and they established themselves as the Order of Shadow. On the night Zed planned to return to the temple, he visited Yevnai one last time and stole a kiss from her, wishing to burn the final bridge to his old life.

In truth, Zed convinced Kusho to fake his death so that the Kinkou's mission could remain pure. They believed a new order had to be born to stop Noxus—one the Kinkou had to despise—so that they would not lose their honor by allying with them. With Kusho's supposed death, they were free to use the Tears' forbidden magic and allowed Kusho to take control of the Navori Brotherhood and guide their nation to safety from behind the scenes. Zed and his order etched their bodies with tattoos from the Tears' ink, allowing them to fight with dark reflections of themselves.

After Noxian forces massacred Ionian defenders at the Epool River, Zed and the Order of Shadow came upon the grisly scene. Among the survivors was a child soldier, no more than ten years old, who leveled his blade against the master assassin. Seeing the pain and fury in the boy's eyes, Zed believed he could shape him into a weapon against those who had sent him here to die. He extended his hand and offered Shieda Kayn a place within the Order of Shadow.

Under Zed's tutelage, Kayn mastered every weapon the Order possessed. As his skill grew, so too did his arrogance, and he came to believe that one day he could eclipse even Zed himself. For Kayn's final test, Zed tasked him with locating a Darkin weapon recently uncovered by Noxus and preventing it from being used against the defenders of Ionia. When Kayn returned, he now wielded the Darkin scythe Rhaast.

When the Noxian invasion finally ended, Zed and his order reached out to the Kinkou, and though he did not reconcile with Shen, they came to an uneasy accord.

children.title: Reuniting with Shen
children.textContent:
Realizing he could not stop Jhin alone, Zed traveled to the Quenjo Estate to seek out his and Shen's former beloved, Yevnai. Her engagement to Shen had been called off after his father's death and she had since married Lord Quenjo. Yevnai's vastaya servant initially tried turning him away, but she invited in. He asked for her help in locating Shen so they could stop Jhin together, but she refused. As he watched Yevnai's children play, Zed noticed one of them having traces of vastayan heritage. After Zed threatened to expose her affair to her husband, Yevnai finally relented and revealed that Shen still wrote to her and had recently mentioned an inn he frequented. Before departing, Zed offered Yevnai protection within one of the Order of Shadow's temples, but she declined. While Zed feared that Jhin might target her, he believed Yevnai was more valuable to him alive, as it pained him more than death that she still lived yet no longer cared for him.

Zed then led his followers to the White Cliffs Inn, a place where Shen liked to stop and rest. Kayn urged his master to eliminate Eye of Twilight so they could return to their work, but Zed refused, believing Shen was the only person capable of helping trap Jhin. He ordered his students to remain outside while he entered the inn alone to confront Shen.

After a waitress brought Shen his meal, he noticed the shadows behaving strangely and drew his spirit blade. He called out to Zed that he knew his students were here and, soon, Zed entered the inn disguised as a merchant. Shen charged at Zed with blade, but he managed to block and evade his attacks. As they fought, Zed revealed that Khada Jhin had escaped. Shen thought it impossible, but Zed reminded him of what he was capable of and told him that they were the only two who could stop him again. Frustrated but wary, Shen lowered his weapon to hear him out. Zed believed Jhin was setting a trap, and that the only way to catch him was for them to work together. Still consumed by anger, Shen refused. Bound by the Code of Tuonn, Shen's honor compelled him to let Zed leave in peace, but he vowed to capture Jhin himself and warned Zed to go into hiding, as when they next meet, he would kill him.

As Zed prepared to leave, Akali stopped him. Shen ordered her to let him leave, but she disregarded him and attacked. Zed effortlessly evaded her strikes, and he and his disciples vanished into the shadows while Shen restrained Akali. Seething with rage, Shen decided to forsake the armistice between the Kinkou Order and the Order of Shadow and declared war on Zed.

After retreating, Zed decided he would shadow Shen as he followed Jhin's trail. Kayn warned him that it would be dangerous, as they were now at war with the Kinkou. Zed agreed, and to minimize risk to the Yánléi, he gathered his disciples and swore in Kayn as his successor before setting off alone.

Zed followed Shen and Akali as they pursued leads on Jhin across the region. They discovered that he had been targeting enemies of the Kashuri faction of the Navori Brotherhood and learned that one of the last holdouts in the area was the Elder of Nanthee. They rushed to the Port of Nanthee to protect him, but arrived too late. One thing Zed knew that Shen did not was that, although the Brotherhood had hired the Order of Shadow for assassinations, they never mandated the killing of innocents. He believed Jhin's murders were meant to taunt him personally. As he moved through Nanthee, Zed noticed something strange about the town's lanterns, they hung with two strings. When he went to investigate, he realized Jhin had turned them into traps.

children.title: Tracking Jhin
children.textContent:
Knowing that Jhin was still in Nanthee waiting to watch his bombs go off, Zed searched for him. He remembered that this town rang a bell four times when a ship was about to depart and realized that was when they would detonate. He finally found Jhin and gave chase after they bell began to ring, but Shen spotted him and pursued. Zed tried to warn Shen that Jhin had rigged the town's lanterns with traps, but he refused to listen. Moments later, Jhin's bombs detonated, triggering explosions throughout the port. Zed shoved Shen into the sea, saving him from the blasts, while Jhin marveled at his work and escaped. As Shen began to drown, Zed dove in after him and dragged him to shore.

When Shen regained consciousness, he drew his blade on Zed. Zed gestured to the carnage around them and pleaded that they needed to work together to stop Jhin. Enraged, Shen charged at him, insisting that Jhin had staged the massacre knowing Zed would be watching, and that if Zed had remained hidden as warned, Jhin would have gone after him instead. Zed tried to tell him that Jhin was working for someone else, but Shen didn't believe him. He knew that Kusho was the one who had released Jhin, yet Zed his oath to his master prevented him from revealing the truth. Instead, he tried to hint at Kusho's identity by reminding Shen of his father's lesson on the rule of the scales, though Shen replied that his lesson had more than one part. He told him that they were taught action only when necessary, and accused Zed of failing the Kinkou—and Kusho—because of his impatience. Shen ordered Zed to stand down, but Zed threatened to kill him with his shadow clones. Shen retorted that the clones were shaped by Zed's hatred and guilt, that they did not serve him but controlled him. Gaining the upper hand, Shen began to beat down on Zed and tearfully denounced him for killing his father, declaring that Zed had once been his brother. With Zed unconscious, Shen ordered the Kinkou to bind him, planning to burn away his shadow magic before leaving him to rot in a cell.

Two days after the attack on the port, Shen had Zed restrained and prepared for transport when he received a message from Akali. Shen confronted Zed and told him that he knew he had been holding back and that he allowed himself to be captured. Zed once again tried to hint at Kusho's idenity by questioning if Shen had discovered who had helped Jhin escape, but Shen admitted that he had not and promised to protect Zed if Jhin came for him. Alternatively, Shen offered Zed one final chance to work together to stop Jhin on the condition that he finally tells him the truth about why he had killed his father.

Zed claimed that he needed the power of the Tears of Shadow to stop the Noxians, and that Master Kusho had tried to stop him. Shen saw through his lie, pointing out that Zed could have taken the Tears without killing Kusho. Zed then confessed that he had been jealous of how much Kusho loved Shen and believed he needed shadow magic to become stronger. Again, Shen saw through his lie, as Kusho loved him just as much, and demanded the truth. At last, Zed admitted that Kusho had been right: if the Kinkou became involved in the war, their sacred mission would be corrupted. To save Ionia, a new order had to be born, but if Zed had merely taken the Tears, members of the Kinkou would have followed him. To sever that possibility, he had to become the Kinkou's hated enemy and betray Master Kusho. Shen accepted Zed's answer and freed him. While Shen wanted to capture Jhin, Zed insisted that he had to die. Shen refused, arguing that Jhin did not threaten the balance, but Zed countered he was not a member of the Kinkou. However, Shen refused to let Zed die thinking their father had been wrong and if he wanted his help, this was the price.

children.title: Stopping the Killer
children.textContent:
As they prepared to depart, Shen informed Zed that Jhin had escaped to Piltover, and that Akali had gone after him. Upon arriving in the City of Progress, they attempted to follow her trail. Zed again pressed Shen on whether he had discovered who had helped Jhin escape, but Shen dismissed it, as the only ones who knew where Jhin was being held were the two of them, Yevnai, and his father. One of Shen's informants eventually guided them an old opera house where Akali had been last spotted. There, they encountered an agent of the Navori Brotherhood who revealed he had led her into Jhin's trap. Shen and Zed then rushed in to save her.

They found Akali on the far side of a bridge, bound to one of Jhin's props and guarded by his traps, while Jhin sat nearby, calmly observing. Shen teleported in to strike him down, but it was only a decoy, and he was caught in a barrage of explosives. The real Jhin then revealed himself and moved to execute Akali, but Zed emerged from the shadows and the two rushed him together. Jhin evaded their attacks and unleashed more traps, incapacitating them both, though Zed managed to free Akali.

As Jhin prepared to finish Shen off, Akali struck, pinning Jhin to the ground and raising his own gun to kill him. Shen pleaded with her to stop, but Zed knocked her aside and demanded to know how Jhin had escaped. Jhin replied that he had not escaped but, rather, he was freed, and that Zed already knew by whom. Enraged, Zed hurled Jhin at Shen's feet and declared their bargain fulfilled before vanishing into the shadows.

Several weeks later, Zed returned to Ionia to confront the Order's patron. As he snuck into his fortress, Zed wished that Shen had picked up on his hints and investigated who had helped Jhin escaped. When he entered, Zed found Kayn at Kusho's side. Zed demanded to know why he had released Jhin. To which Kusho justified his actions by claiming that the fear Jhin inspired was necessary to unite the nation under the Navori Brotherhood. Kusho also knew that Zed had been trying to point Shen in his direction by getting him to figure out how Jhin escaped. Refusing to let his former master plunge Ionia into civil war, Zed turned against him. Kusho ordered Kayn to kill Zed, and Kayn raised his scythe, yet he remained loyal to Zed, revealing that those still loyal to him were waiting nearby. Zed ordered Kayn to deal with the remaining traitors while he confronted Kusho alone.

Kusho consumed the Tears of Shadow, transforming into a monstrous, living shadow, and attacked. He taunted Zed with his failures, but Zed had long since embraced his own darkness. In the end, he struck Kusho down. Holding his former master as he died, Zed realized he had finally committed the very crime he had once been accused of. Outside, Kayn had dispatched the traitors and Kusho's soldiers, but more were coming. He was incredulous that Kusho really thought he would betray Zed after he saved him and gave him a home with the Order. As they left, Zed wished Shen had picked up on his clues regarding Kusho, but was happy they had at least recaptured Jhin.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Zed is a muscular but slimly built man with brown eyes, fair skin, and shaggy white hair. His signature armor is a menacing ninja regalia, draped with dark red and blue fabrics tucked into dark gray metal with golden accents. The most notable feature is a sinister metal mask with vertical slats over the mouth. Zed's eyes seem to glow red from within. His weapons of choice are a pair of retractable, dual-tipped blades that extend from his forearms. He also carries 2 large shuriken on his back,

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Like many Ionians, Zed is deeply dedicated to his homeland. Where he differs, however, is how far he is willing to go in order to protect it. Ruthless and decisive, Zed is willing to master forbidden arts, utilize deception and secrecy, and even betray his allies in his efforts to keep Ionia safe.

children.title: Shen
children.textContent: During their training, Shen and Zed were as close as brothers. However, their relationship was shattered when Zed seemingly killed Kusho and the other Kinkou during his coup. Despite this, Zed still considered Shen the greatest friendship he ever had and sought his aid to hunt down Jhin.

children.title: Jhin
children.textContent: Zed despises Jhin for the horrors he had inflicted on his fellow Ionians. He wished to kill him after they apprehended him, but was stopped by Kusho. Years later, when Jhin escaped, Zed worked with Shen to hunt him down again.

children.title: Kayn
children.textContent: Zed found Kayn when he was 10 year old, and took him in as an apprentice. For saving and training him, Kayn became deeply loyal to Zed, but one day wishes to succeed his master as the head of the Order of Shadow.

mainContent.textContent:
